realWorldExample: This is how baking soda volcanoes work at science fairs.
funFact: The gas produced is the same CO₂ in your fizzy drinks.

realWorldExample: This is how industrial waste water is neutralized before disposal.
funFact: The product, sodium chloride, is ordinary table salt.

realWorldExample: A famous chemistry demonstration performed worldwide.
funFact: The foam is just soap bubbles filled with pure oxygen.

realWorldExample: Used in Fehling's solution to test for sugars in food.
funFact: The vivid blue precipitate is copper hydroxide — same compound that gives the Statue of Liberty its color.

realWorldExample: How iron is dissolved in industrial metal processing.
funFact: The bubbles are hydrogen gas — the same gas used in fuel cells.
